mnemonics.title: Procurement Process Steps
mnemonics.content: I RAISE VC: Identify Requirements → Research vendors → Analyze options → Select vendor → Execute contract → Validate delivery → Evaluate performance → Close/Dispose

mnemonics.title: Vendor Evaluation Criteria
mnemonics.content: CRQFT: Cost, Reliability, Quality, Financial stability, Technical capability

mnemonics.title: Make vs Buy Factors
mnemonics.content: CODES: Cost analysis, Organizational fit, Development time, Expertise available, Strategic importance

acronyms.usage: Early stage document to gather vendor capabilities and product information before formal bidding
Formal document soliciting detailed proposals including pricing, implementation plan, and technical specifications
Document requesting specific pricing for well-defined products or services
Contract defining expected service standards, uptime guarantees, and penalties for non-compliance
Complete lifecycle cost of IT assets including acquisition, operation, maintenance, and disposal
Cloud model providing virtualized computing resources like servers, storage, networking
Cloud model providing development and deployment platforms without infrastructure management
Cloud model delivering software applications over internet on subscription basis
Complete lifecycle cost of IT assets including acquisition, operation, maintenance, and disposal

visualTips.visualization: Think of an iceberg - visible purchase price is just the tip, while hidden costs (maintenance, training, downtime, upgrades) form the massive underwater portion
Use a scale - on one side: control, customization, IP; on other side: cost, speed, expertise gaps. Tilt determines decision
Create a grid with vendors as columns and criteria (cost, quality, support) as rows. Score each cell to visually compare options
Circular diagram showing continuous cycle: Requirements → Selection → Execution → Management → Disposal → Back to Requirements

analogies.analogy: Like shopping for a car - you research options (RFI), compare detailed proposals (RFP), negotiate price (RFQ), sign service agreement (SLA), and maintain the vehicle (contract management)
Like being in a gym membership with expensive proprietary equipment - you can only use their gear, and switching would mean abandoning your investment
Like owning a pet - initial cost is small compared to food, vet bills, grooming, and supplies over its lifetime
Like a warranty or insurance policy - promises specific outcomes with penalties if promises are broken
